extra.reasoning: The lock advances stage 0->1 on key_2, 1->2 on key_1, 2->3 on key_1, 3->4 on key_2, 4->5 on key_1, 5->6 on key_0, so the unlock combination is key_2,key_1,key_1,key_2,key_1,key_0. The advance transitions (adv_*) have no opted_out guard, and t_optout sets opted_out:=true and sent:=false but does NOT reset stage. The vulnerability is t_resume_glitch: on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage>=6) it sets sent:=true. The protective t_send_blocked only covers stage<6. Trace from initial (opted_out=F, sent=F, stage=0): key_2->stage1; key_1->stage2; key_1->stage3; key_2->stage4; key_1->stage5; key_0->stage6 (each adv sets sent=F); optout->opted_out=T, sent=F, stage stays 6; send_attempt->matches t_resume_glitch (opted_out and stage>=6) so sent:=true. Final state opted_out=T and sent=T violates globally(opted_out implies not sent). The sequence is 8 events, within the 9-event bound. Property is VIOLATED.
